Let $\uparrow$ be the binary propositional connective, defined by the following truth table:

|   $P$    |   $Q$    | $P\uparrow Q$ |
| :------: | :------: | :-----------: |
| $\true$  | $\true$  |   $\false$    |
| $\true$  | $\false$ |   $\false$    |
| $\false$ | $\true$  |   $\false$    |
| $\false$ | $\false$ |    $\true$    |

a. Show that the operations $\neg$ and $\conj$ are both expressible in terms of
$\uparrow$.

b. Write a sentence that is logically equivalent to $P\imp Q$ that uses only the
connective $\uparrow$.
